description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, sign, deploy, staging, and production that runs a global before_script to unset proxies, list CI variables, prepend a custom bin directory to PATH, set CI_BUILDPKG_BOOTSTRAP=yes, export PATH and CI_BUILDPKG_BOOTSTRAP, and execute ./gitlab-buildpkg-tools/install-dependencies.sh before every job. In the build stage define many jobs (build_centos_6, build_centos_7, build_centos_8, build_debian_bookworm, build_debian_bullseye, build_debian_buster, build_debian_jessie, build_debian_stretch, build_fedora_24‑30, build_ubuntu_bionic, build_ubuntu_focal, build_ubuntu_jammy, build_ubuntu_kinetic, build_ubuntu_lunar) each using its respective buildpkg/* Docker image, tagged with docker, producing artifacts that expire in 1 day containing result/*, and running the single script command "ci-build-pkg". Add a sign job in the sign stage that depends on all build jobs, uses the buildpkg/debian:stretch image, runs "cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR && ci-sign-pkg", and stores the same result/* artifacts for 1 day, executing only on the master branch. Add a pages job in the deploy stage with the buildpkg/debian:stretch image, variables PAGES_DIR=public and PAGES_HOST=gitlab.io, runs "cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR && ci-pages-ppa && ci-pages-home && ci-pages-tree", publishes the public/ directory as an artifact, runs only on master but skips when only README.md, LICENSE, or tests change, and creates a PPA environment. Finally, create manual deploy_staging and deploy_production jobs in the staging and production stages respectively, both using docker‑tagged runners, the former with image buildpkg/ubuntu:xenial running "cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR && ci-deploy-pkg staging", the latter with image buildpkg/debian:stretch running "cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR && ci-deploy-pkg production", each limited to the master branch and dependent on prior jobs as specified.
